ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT BAYER CROPSCIENCE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PARTIES SUBMITTED TO THE TENTATIVE RULING. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT BAYER CROPSCIENCE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 (NEGLIGENCE) AND 2 (STRICT LIABILITY) AND GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 3 (FALSE REPRESENTATION) AND 4 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES). THE DECLARATIONS OF MR. HAASE AND MR. MINSTER AND DEFENDANT'S GO 129 INTERROGATORY RESPONSES CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MRS. Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Carrie Phillips, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BAYER CROPSCIENCE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Parties submitted to the tentative ruling. Tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence) and 2 (strict liability) and granted as to issues 3 (false representation) and 4 (punitive damages). The declarations of Mr. Haase and Mr. Minster and defendant's GO 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Mrs. McClure was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Foster's fibrous adhesive) attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Sage's declaration are sustained. Defendants' objections to the declarations of Mr. Haase and Mr. Minster are overruled. Plaintiffs' concede the lack of merit of their claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
